definition: The Dollar Smile framework posits three collectively exhaustive regimes (risk-off dollar strength, risk-on dollar weakness, US exceptionalism dollar strength) but lacks ex-ante regime identification rules with documented hit rates. Historical counterexamples (2017-18 US outperformance with dollar weakness; Q2-Q3 2020 risk-off with dollar weakness) are dismissed as transitional rather than treated as framework failures.
evidence: No ex-ante regime identification rule with documented hit rates exists
Counterexamples dismissed as transitional rather than framework failures
pair_3 judged the unfalsifiability critique logically airtight
Fiscal smirk modification adds another degree of freedom
Meese-Rogoff (1983, replicated) shows random walk beats all FX models at short horizons
fx_strategist_01 doesn't even try to defend it
open_questions: Can the framework be made falsifiable with stated ex-ante regime calls and hit rates?
Does the fiscal smirk modification improve or degrade analytical value?
Is descriptive usefulness sufficient justification for continued use?
